input.content:
---
id: bloomberg-deep-history-2026-06-04
title: Bloomberg OHLCV rotation fix + make-current + deep-history backfill (2026-06-04)
kind: docs
status: in-flight
terminal: lab
pr:
decisions: []
date: 2026-06-04
headline: Fixed the OHLCV column-rotation defect, made all Tier-1 + context data current to 2026-06-04, deepened all single-series context to inception, generalized the pullers to contiguous backfill, and began the per-name deep-history backfill (vol_iv incl. the 2008 GFC) — deep data routed to a gz buffer branch to keep the connector monoliths <100 MB.
surface: [scripts/_bbg_panel.py, scripts/pull_ohlcv.py, scripts/pull_liquidity.py, scripts/pull_vol_iv.py, scripts/pull_vix_term_structure.py, scripts/pull_context_index.py, data/bloomberg/]
---

## Goal

Execute the deep-history + make-current pull from the transient lab terminal:
FIX the OHLCV rotation defect, make every Tier-1/context series current to
2026-06-04, deepen cheap context to inception, and backfill the expensive
per-name panels (OHLCV/IV/liquidity) toward the 1994 IV floor, newest-first and
contiguous, committing each chunk. Data only — **no engine/connector edits**.

## Environment

`C:\Anaconda3\python.exe` (3.13.9) venv at `C:\Users\mertmert\bbg-venv`;
`blpapi 3.26.4.2`, `xbbg 1.2.6`, `pandas 3.0.3`; `PYTHONUTF8=1`. API entitled.
git identity `Mert Yakar <mertyakar.my@gmail.com>`.

## STORAGE ARCHITECTURE (operator decision — important)

GitHub's HARD file limit is **100 MB** (the earlier 50 MB notes were soft
warnings). The deep per-name backfill pushes the monoliths past 100 MB, so:

- **Refresh branch `data/bloomberg-refresh-2026-06-02`** keeps the
  connector-read MONOLITHS (`sp500_ohlcv.csv`, `sp500_liquidity.csv`,
  `sp500_vol_iv_full.csv`) **FROZEN at their <100 MB pushed floors** (vol_iv at
  the 2012-07-02 floor, 94.5 MB). The engine baseline stays clean & pushable.
- **Deep history goes to SEPARATE gz files** under `data/bloomberg/deep/`, pushed
  to a transient **buffer branch `deep-history/bloomberg-raw`** (NEVER merged).
  gz keeps them well under 100 MB (the 2007–2012 vol_iv slice is 8.7 MB gz).
- NOT Git LFS (quota/cost). The permanent home is **Google Drive**, set up
  later from the laptop (see DEFER).

## What was done

### Phase 1 — OHLCV rotation defect FIXED (commit 8fdf612, pushed)
Connector (`engine/data_connector.py:202-208`) renames `open->high, high->close,
close->open`; the stored CSV must be ROTATED (stored `open` = true HIGH/row-max,
stored `low` = true LOW). The prior 2026-03-23+ delta was CANONICAL → inverted.
Fix: restored the clean rotated base from origin/main (->2026-03-20), re-pulled
2026-03-23->2026-06-04 with field map `{PX_HIGH->open, PX_LAST->high,
PX_LOW->low, PX_OPEN->close, PX_VOLUME->volume}`. Post-build GATE: **0 violations
across 1,014,916 non-NaN rows**. Verified offline (round-trip) + live.

### Phase 2 — make-current + cheap context to inception (all pushed)
- **2a Tier-1 current (8fdf612):** ohlcv re-pulled to 06-04; liquidity + vol_iv
  to 06-04.
- **2b vix_term_structure (e386a51):** 2018->03-20 → **1990-01-02 -> 2026-06-04**
  (9,200 rows; vix_3m 2002, vix_6m 2008).
- **2d context indices to inception (ccd821d):**
  - `vix_futures_curve` 20,111 -> **36,779** (UX1-7 from 2004-03-26)
  - `vol_indices` 22,968 -> **43,244** (SKEW 1990 … VXEEM/VIX9D 2011)
  - `spx_correlation` 8,605 -> **15,396** (COR1M/3M/6M from 2006)
  - `rates_fx_vol` 8,716 -> **24,842** (MOVE 1988 / JPMVXYG7 1992 / CVIX 2001)

### Phase 3a — per-name deep backfill (begun; newest-first; floor 1994)
- **window 1 (8dc6f59, pushed in the refresh monolith):** vol_iv 2012-07 ->
  2015-01 (+280,302). This is the frozen floor of the refresh-branch monolith
  (vol_iv now 2012-07-02 -> 2026-06-04, 1,668,021 rows, 94.5 MB).
- **windows 2-3 (relocated to the gz buffer branch):** vol_iv 2007-07 -> 2012-06
  (+526,932). **The 2008 GFC IV is captured.** Stored as
  `data/bloomberg/deep/sp500_vol_iv_full__1994_2012.csv.gz` (526,932 rows,
  2007-07-02 -> 2012-06-29, 436 tickers, 8.7 MB gz) on
  `deep-history/bloomberg-raw`. The full uncompressed scratch monolith
  (2007-07 -> 2026-06-04) is also at `C:\Users\mertmert\deep_scratch\` on the box.

## The machinery

`scripts/_bbg_panel.py` (NEW) — shared engine. Fills FORWARD gap [max+1 -> END]
then walks BACKWARD [min-1 -> FLOOR] **newest-first in ~30-month windows**,
rewriting after EVERY window (durable). Per-window: pull members in 30-ticker
`bdh` -> pivot long->wide -> merge+dedupe(date,ticker keep-last) -> sort -> write
-> optional validate() (the OHLCV gate). Pullers are thin configs.

Env knobs: `SWE_PULL_END`, `SWE_PULL_FLOOR`, `SWE_PULL_MODE`
(forward|backfill|both), `SWE_BACKFILL_CHUNK_MONTHS`, `SWE_BACKFILL_MAX_WINDOWS`,
`SWE_OUT_PATH` (write to a scratch path instead of the monolith — used for deep
backfill), `SWE_PULL_LIMIT`, `SWE_PULL_NO_WRITE`.

Cheap-context pullers: `scripts/pull_vix_term_structure.py`,
`scripts/pull_context_index.py <dataset>` (per-ticker pulls so one bad ticker
can't null the batch). Offline tests (`test_pullers_offline.py`, kept off-repo)
prove window tiling + the rotation round-trip/gate. All passed.

## Gotchas

- xbbg 1.2.6 → long narwhals; `.to_native()` + `pivot_table`; `PYTHONUTF8=1`.
- Historical windows use **today's** SPX members (post-IPO names absent in old
  windows; departed names keep old rows). Survivorship is inherent.
- Meter: ~3-6M points this session, **zero throttling** (one 30-month per-name
  window ≈ 1.9M points). Full 3-panel backfill to 1994 ≈ 20-40M → will hit the
  cap; pull-until-error, per-window write keeps what landed, resume.

## RESUME RUNBOOK

Terminal up + logged in; venv active; `cd` repo; `$env:PYTHONUTF8='1'`.

### Continue the deep backfill (vol_iv shown; ohlcv/liquidity analogous)
Grow the off-monolith scratch with `SWE_OUT_PATH`, then re-carve the gz and push
to the buffer branch. The refresh-branch monolith is NEVER advanced.
```
$env:SWE_OUT_PATH='C:\Users\mertmert\deep_scratch\sp500_vol_iv_full.csv'
$env:SWE_PULL_MODE='backfill'; $env:SWE_BACKFILL_MAX_WINDOWS='2'
python scripts/pull_vol_iv.py            # grows scratch 2004..2007, etc.
# re-carve deep slice (date < 2012-07-02) -> data/bloomberg/deep/*.csv.gz, then:
git checkout deep-history/bloomberg-raw
git add -f data/bloomberg/deep/sp500_vol_iv_full__1994_2012.csv.gz
git commit -m "deep: vol_iv backfill chunk" ; git push origin deep-history/bloomberg-raw
git checkout data/bloomberg-refresh-2026-06-02
```
For OHLCV/liquidity: first SEED the scratch from the frozen monolith
(`copy data\bloomberg\sp500_ohlcv.csv <scratch>`), then same loop with
`SWE_OUT_PATH=<scratch>` and a deep filename `sp500_ohlcv__1994_2018.csv.gz` /
`sp500_liquidity__1994_2015.csv.gz`. (OHLCV scratch keeps the rotated map; its
gate still applies.)

### Not done this session (lower urgency / bespoke)
- **Phase 2c snapshots** (`sp500_fundamentals`, `credit_risk`, `institutional`,
  `analyst`, `iv_snapshot_today`): point-in-time `bdp`; re-pull anytime with the
  prior session's verified field lists.
- **sp500_macro:** stale to 2026-03-20; OHLC-per-instrument; refresh + backfill
  to 1990 (+ IG/HY OAS + financial-conditions proxy if entitled). Verify the
  per-instrument bbg map on one instrument first.
- **Phase 4 IV moneyness/skew surface:** `sp500_iv_surface.csv`,
  `{30,60,90}DAY_IMPVOL_{90,95,100,105,110}.0%MNY_DF`, LONG
  `date,ticker,tenor_days,moneyness_pct,iv`. Verify the field floor on one
  ticker; prioritise put side + crisis windows.

## DEFER to laptop/overnight (NOT the metered terminal)
Move the deep gz files from `deep-history/bloomberg-raw` into **Google Drive**
(permanent home); convert to Parquet partitioned by year; add the connector
read-path that assembles recent(git monolith) + deep(Drive); then delete the gz
buffer branch. That connector wiring is the real fix and is out of scope here.
Also deferred (wrong channel): option chains (Theta), dealer GEX (subscription),
`borrow_rate_net` + macro `importance`/deep-history (BQL/Excel), `treasury_yields`
(yfinance).

content.content: The file C:\Users\mertmert\smart-wheel-engine\docs\worklog\bloomberg-deep-history-2026-06-04.md has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

Terminal up + logged in; venv active; `cd` repo; `$env:PYTHONUTF8='1'`.

### Continue the deep backfill (vol_iv shown; ohlcv/liquidity analogous)
Grow the off-monolith scratch with `SWE_OUT_PATH`, then re-carve the gz and push
to the buffer branch. The refresh-branch monolith is NEVER advanced.
```
$env:SWE_OUT_PATH='C:\Users\mertmert\deep_scratch\sp500_vol_iv_full.csv'
$env:SWE_PULL_MODE='backfill'; $env:SWE_BACKFILL_MAX_WINDOWS='2'
python scripts/pull_vol_iv.py            # grows scratch 2004..2007, etc.
# re-carve deep slice (date < 2012-07-02) -> data/bloomberg/deep/*.csv.gz, then:

git checkout data/bloomberg-refresh-2026-06-02
```

(`copy data\bloomberg\sp500_ohlcv.csv <scratch>`), then same loop with
`SWE_OUT_PATH=<scratch>` and a deep filename `sp500_ohlcv__1994_2018.csv.gz` /
`sp500_liquidity__1994_2015.csv.gz`. (OHLCV scratch keeps the rotated map; its
gate still applies.)

### Not done this session (lower urgency / bespoke)
- **Phase 2c snapshots** (`sp500_fundamentals`, `credit_risk`, `institutional`,

  `date,ticker,tenor_days,moneyness_pct,iv`. Verify the field floor on one
  ticker; prioritise put side + crisis windows.

## DEFER to laptop/overnight (NOT the metered terminal)
Move the deep gz files from `deep-history/bloomberg-raw` into **Google Drive**
(permanent home); convert to Parquet partitioned by year; add the connector
read-path that assembles recent(git monolith) + deep(Drive); then delete the gz
buffer branch. That connector wiring is the real fix and is out of scope here.
Also deferred (wrong channel): option chains (Theta), dealer GEX (subscription),
`borrow_rate_net` + macro `importance`/deep-history (BQL/Excel), `treasury_yields`
(yfinance).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
---
id: bloomberg-deep-history-2026-06-04
title: Bloomberg OHLCV rotation fix + make-current + deep-history backfill (2026-06-04)
kind: docs
status: in-flight
terminal: lab
pr:
decisions: []
date: 2026-06-04
headline: Fixed the OHLCV column-rotation defect, made all Tier-1 + context data current to 2026-06-04, deepened all single-series context to inception, generalized the pullers to contiguous backfill, and began the per-name deep-history backfill (vol_iv) toward the 1994 floor.
surface: [scripts/_bbg_panel.py, scripts/pull_ohlcv.py, scripts/pull_liquidity.py, scripts/pull_vol_iv.py, scripts/pull_vix_term_structure.py, scripts/pull_context_index.py, data/bloomberg/]
---

## Goal

Execute the deep-history + make-current pull from the transient lab terminal:
(1) FIX the known OHLCV column-rotation defect, (2) make every Tier-1 and
context series current to today (2026-06-04), (3) deepen history — cheap
single-series context to inception, and the expensive per-name panels
(OHLCV/IV/liquidity) backward toward the 1994 IV floor, newest-first and
contiguous, committing each chunk. Data only — **no engine/connector edits**.

## Environment

Prep verified earlier (see readiness report). `C:\Anaconda3\python.exe` (3.13.9)
venv at `C:\Users\mertmert\bbg-venv`; `blpapi 3.26.4.2`, `xbbg 1.2.6`,
`pandas 3.0.3`; `PYTHONUTF8=1`. API entitled (SPX 7584 / AAPL 311 at prep).
git identity set to `Mert Yakar <mertyakar.my@gmail.com>`.

## What was done

### Phase 1 — OHLCV rotation defect FIXED (commit 8fdf612)
The connector (`engine/data_connector.py:202-208`) renames `open->high,
high->close, close->open`, so the stored CSV must use a ROTATED layout where
stored `open` holds the true HIGH (row-max) and stored `low` the true LOW. The
prior delta (2026-03-23+) had been written CANONICAL, inverting price for the
engine.

Fix: `git checkout origin/main -- data/bloomberg/sp500_ohlcv.csv` to recover the
clean rotated base (->2026-03-20), then re-pull 2026-03-23->2026-06-04 with a
corrected field map `{PX_HIGH->open, PX_LAST->high, PX_LOW->low, PX_OPEN->close,
PX_VOLUME->volume}`. A post-build GATE asserts every row has stored
`open==max(o,h,l,c)` and `low==min(...)`: **0 violations across 1,014,916
non-NaN rows.** Verified the round-trip offline (stored layout -> connector
rename -> correct OHLC) and live (AAPL 2026-03-23 stored open=254.56=row-max).

### Phase 2 — make-current + cheap context to inception
- **2a Tier-1 current (8fdf612):** liquidity + vol_iv extended 06-03->06-04.
- **2b vix_term_structure (e386a51):** VIX/VIX3M/VIX6M PX_LAST; was 2018->03-20
  (stale), now **1990-01-02 -> 2026-06-04** (9,200 rows). vix_3m from 2002,
  vix_6m from 2008.
- **2d context indices to inception (ccd821d):** per-series PX_LAST.
  - `vix_futures_curve` 20,111 -> **36,779** (UX1-7, 2004-03-26 ->)
  - `vol_indices` 22,968 -> **43,244** (SKEW 1990 / VXN 2001 / RVX 2004 /
    VVIX 2006 / OVX 2007 / GVZ 2008 / VXEEM+VIX9D 2011 ->)
  - `spx_correlation` 8,605 -> **15,396** (COR1M/3M/6M, 2006-01-03 ->)
  - `rates_fx_vol` 8,716 -> **24,842** (MOVE 1988 / JPMVXYG7 1992 / CVIX 2001 ->)

### Phase 3 — per-name deep backfill (begun, newest-first, floor 1994)
- **3a vol_iv window 1 (8dc6f59):** 2012-07 -> 2015-01, +280,302 rows.
- **3a vol_iv windows 2-3:** 2012-07 -> 2007-07 (+526,932 rows, no throttling).
  vol_iv now **2,194,953 rows, 2007-07-02 -> 2026-06-04** — the **2008 GFC IV is
  captured**. Backfill continues toward the 1994 floor (~5 more windows).

## The machinery (generalized pullers)

`scripts/_bbg_panel.py` (NEW) — shared engine for the three per-name pullers.
Given an existing CSV it fills the FORWARD gap [max+1 -> END] then walks the
BACKWARD gap [min-1 -> FLOOR] **newest-first in ~30-month windows**, rewriting
the CSV after EVERY window (durable: a metered cap mid-run loses nothing and
coverage stays hole-free from the recent edge). Per-window: pull all members in
30-ticker `bdh` calls -> pivot long->wide -> merge+dedupe(date,ticker, keep-last)
-> sort -> write -> optional validate() (the OHLCV gate). The three pullers
(`pull_ohlcv.py`, `pull_liquidity.py`, `pull_vol_iv.py`) are now thin configs.

Env knobs: `SWE_PULL_END`, `SWE_PULL_FLOOR`, `SWE_PULL_MODE` (forward|backfill|
both), `SWE_BACKFILL_CHUNK_MONTHS`, `SWE_BACKFILL_MAX_WINDOWS` (cap backward
windows per run — used for per-chunk commits), `SWE_PULL_LIMIT`,
`SWE_PULL_NO_WRITE`.

New cheap-context pullers: `scripts/pull_vix_term_structure.py` and
`scripts/pull_context_index.py <dataset>` (per-ticker pulls so one bad ticker
can't null the batch).

Offline correctness test (`test_pullers_offline.py`, kept outside the repo):
proves window tiling is contiguous/no-overlap/newest-first/floor-clamped, and
the rotation field-map<->connector round-trip + gate. All passed.

## Gotchas / notes for the next agent

- **xbbg 1.2.6 returns long-format narwhals** — handled via `.to_native()` +
  `pivot_table`. `PYTHONUTF8=1` is mandatory (narwhals repr crashes cp1252).
- **Current-members survivorship:** historical windows use *today's* SPX
  constituents (post-2014 IPOs are correctly absent in older windows; former
  members that left the index keep their old rows but stop at exit).
- **The big CSVs keep growing** — vol_iv ~94 MB and rising ~13 MB per backfill
  window; ohlcv ~60 MB, liquidity ~67 MB. GitHub warns (>50 MB) but accepts
  (no LFS). A full backfill to 1994 will push vol_iv toward ~250 MB. Consider
  migrating these to Git LFS or Parquet before the full deep pull.
- **Meter:** ~2.5-6M data points pulled this session with **zero throttling**,
  so the lab cap has comfortable headroom above one 30-month per-name window
  (~1.9M points). A full 3-panel backfill to 1994 is ~20-40M points and will
  likely hit the cap — pull until it errors, the per-window write keeps what
  landed, commit, resume.

## Unresolved / handoff — RESUME RUNBOOK

Bloomberg Terminal running + logged in, venv active, `cd` to repo, then
`$env:PYTHONUTF8='1'`. Each command auto-detects the existing CSV and pulls only
the missing window(s). **Commit + push after each run.**

**Finish vol_iv backfill to 1994** (repeat until "Nothing to pull"):
```
$env:SWE_PULL_MODE='backfill'; $env:SWE_BACKFILL_MAX_WINDOWS='2'
python scripts/pull_vol_iv.py
git add data/bloomberg/sp500_vol_iv_full.csv && git commit -m "data: vol_iv backfill" && git push origin HEAD:data/bloomberg-refresh-2026-06-02
```

**OHLCV deep backfill** (existing min 2018-01-02 -> 1994; uses the corrected
rotated map; gate runs after each window):
```
$env:SWE_PULL_MODE='backfill'; $env:SWE_BACKFILL_MAX_WINDOWS='2'
python scripts/pull_ohlcv.py   # then commit+push
```

**Liquidity deep backfill** (existing min 2015-01-02 -> 1994; VOLUME_AVG_30D,
TURNOVER, EQY_SH_OUT):
```
$env:SWE_PULL_MODE='backfill'; $env:SWE_BACKFILL_MAX_WINDOWS='2'
python scripts/pull_liquidity.py   # then commit+push
```

**Phase 2c — re-snap snapshots (not done):** `sp500_fundamentals`,
`sp500_credit_risk`, `sp500_institutional`, `sp500_analyst`,
`sp500_iv_snapshot_today` — point-in-time `bdp` snapshots; re-pull anytime with
the prior session's verified field lists. Low urgency (trivially re-pullable).

**sp500_macro (not done):** stale to 2026-03-20; OHLC-per-instrument schema;
refresh + backfill to 1990 (+ IG/HY OAS + financial-conditions proxy if
entitled). Needs the per-instrument bbg-ticker map verified on one instrument
first.

**Phase 4 — new per-name IV moneyness/skew surface (not started):**
`sp500_iv_surface.csv`, fields `{30,60,90}DAY_IMPVOL_{90,95,100,105,110}.0%MNY_DF`,
LONG schema `date,ticker,tenor_days,moneyness_pct,iv`. Verify the field floor on
one ticker first; prioritise put side (90/95/100%) + crisis windows.

**Deferred (wrong channel):** option chains (Theta), dealer GEX (subscription),
`borrow_rate_net` + macro `importance`/deep-history (BQL/Excel from home),
`treasury_yields` (yfinance).

While we wait for that, I want to setup google drive access. SET UP DRIVE TRANSFER via rclone (operator does only the browser-authorize click).
1) Download rclone (portable, no admin), in PowerShell:
   cd $env:USERPROFILE
   Invoke-WebRequest https://downloads.rclone.org/rclone-current-windows-amd64.zip -OutFile rclone.zip
   Expand-Archive rclone.zip -DestinationPath rclone -Force
   $rc = (Get-ChildItem rclone -Recurse -Filter rclone.exe | Select-Object -First 1).FullName
   (add its folder to PATH, or use & $rc each time)
2) Configure a Drive remote named "gdrive":   & $rc config
   n -> name: gdrive -> storage: drive -> client_id/secret blank -> scope 1 ->
   root blank -> service_account blank -> advanced n -> auto config y
   >> PAUSE for the operator to authorize in the browser that opens, then y -> q.
3) Test it (must list t.txt before trusting it):
   & $rc mkdir gdrive:swe-deep-history
   "ok" | Out-File -Encoding ascii t.txt
   & $rc copy t.txt gdrive:swe-deep-history
   & $rc ls gdrive:swe-deep-history
4) Destination for deep files = gdrive:swe-deep-history/ . After copying EACH deep file:
   & $rc copy <file> gdrive:swe-deep-history/ ; then & $rc size gdrive:swe-deep-history/
   Confirm the bytes landed on Drive BEFORE deleting any local copy. Never delete local until Drive confirms.
5) If rclone auth fails or the box blocks it, fall back to the gz buffer branch — we lose nothing either way.

import os
import pandas as pd

MONO = r"C:\Users\mertmert\smart-wheel-engine\data\bloomberg\sp500_vol_iv_full.csv"
DEEP_DIR = r"C:\Users\mertmert\smart-wheel-engine\data\bloomberg\deep"
GZ = os.path.join(DEEP_DIR, "sp500_vol_iv_full__1994_2012.csv.gz")
FLOOR_BOUNDARY = "2012-07-02"  # monolith (refresh branch) keeps >= this; deep keeps < this

os.makedirs(DEEP_DIR, exist_ok=True)
df = pd.read_csv(MONO, dtype={"date": str})
print(f"working monolith: {len(df):,} rows, {df['date'].min()} -> {df['date'].max()}")

deep = df[df["date"] < FLOOR_BOUNDARY].copy()
deep = deep.sort_values(["ticker", "date"]).reset_index(drop=True)
deep.to_csv(GZ, index=False, compression="gzip")
sz = os.path.getsize(GZ) / (1024 * 1024)
print(f"DEEP slice (date < {FLOOR_BOUNDARY}): {len(deep):,} rows, "
      f"{deep['date'].min()} -> {deep['date'].max()}, {deep['ticker'].nunique()} tickers")
print(f"wrote {GZ}  ({sz:.1f} MB gz)")

mono_keep = df[df["date"] >= FLOOR_BOUNDARY]
print(f"monolith keeps (date >= {FLOOR_BOUNDARY}): {len(mono_keep):,} rows, "
      f"{mono_keep['date'].min()} -> {mono_keep['date'].max()}  (= the pushed 8dc6f59 floor)")

---
id: bloomberg-deep-history-2026-06-04
title: Bloomberg OHLCV rotation fix + make-current + deep-history backfill (2026-06-04)
kind: docs
status: in-flight
terminal: lab
pr:
decisions: []
date: 2026-06-04
headline: Fixed the OHLCV column-rotation defect, made all Tier-1 + context data current to 2026-06-04, deepened all single-series context to inception, generalized the pullers to contiguous backfill, and began the per-name deep-history backfill (vol_iv incl. the 2008 GFC) — deep data routed to a gz buffer branch to keep the connector monoliths <100 MB.
surface: [scripts/_bbg_panel.py, scripts/pull_ohlcv.py, scripts/pull_liquidity.py, scripts/pull_vol_iv.py, scripts/pull_vix_term_structure.py, scripts/pull_context_index.py, data/bloomberg/]
---

## Goal

Execute the deep-history + make-current pull from the transient lab terminal:
FIX the OHLCV rotation defect, make every Tier-1/context series current to
2026-06-04, deepen cheap context to inception, and backfill the expensive
per-name panels (OHLCV/IV/liquidity) toward the 1994 IV floor, newest-first and
contiguous, committing each chunk. Data only — **no engine/connector edits**.

## Environment

`C:\Anaconda3\python.exe` (3.13.9) venv at `C:\Users\mertmert\bbg-venv`;
`blpapi 3.26.4.2`, `xbbg 1.2.6`, `pandas 3.0.3`; `PYTHONUTF8=1`. API entitled.
git identity `Mert Yakar <mertyakar.my@gmail.com>`.

## STORAGE ARCHITECTURE (operator decision — important)

GitHub's HARD file limit is **100 MB** (the earlier 50 MB notes were soft
warnings). The deep per-name backfill pushes the monoliths past 100 MB, so:

- **Refresh branch `data/bloomberg-refresh-2026-06-02`** keeps the
  connector-read MONOLITHS (`sp500_ohlcv.csv`, `sp500_liquidity.csv`,
  `sp500_vol_iv_full.csv`) **FROZEN at their <100 MB pushed floors** (vol_iv at
  the 2012-07-02 floor, 94.5 MB). The engine baseline stays clean & pushable.
- **Deep history goes to SEPARATE gz files** under `data/bloomberg/deep/`, pushed
  to a transient **buffer branch `deep-history/bloomberg-raw`** (NEVER merged).
  gz keeps them well under 100 MB (the 2007–2012 vol_iv slice is 8.7 MB gz).
- NOT Git LFS (quota/cost). The permanent home is **Google Drive**, set up
  later from the laptop (see DEFER).

## What was done

### Phase 1 — OHLCV rotation defect FIXED (commit 8fdf612, pushed)
Connector (`engine/data_connector.py:202-208`) renames `open->high, high->close,
close->open`; the stored CSV must be ROTATED (stored `open` = true HIGH/row-max,
stored `low` = true LOW). The prior 2026-03-23+ delta was CANONICAL → inverted.
Fix: restored the clean rotated base from origin/main (->2026-03-20), re-pulled
2026-03-23->2026-06-04 with field map `{PX_HIGH->open, PX_LAST->high,
PX_LOW->low, PX_OPEN->close, PX_VOLUME->volume}`. Post-build GATE: **0 violations
across 1,014,916 non-NaN rows**. Verified offline (round-trip) + live.

### Phase 2 — make-current + cheap context to inception (all pushed)
- **2a Tier-1 current (8fdf612):** ohlcv re-pulled to 06-04; liquidity + vol_iv
  to 06-04.
- **2b vix_term_structure (e386a51):** 2018->03-20 → **1990-01-02 -> 2026-06-04**
  (9,200 rows; vix_3m 2002, vix_6m 2008).
- **2d context indices to inception (ccd821d):**
  - `vix_futures_curve` 20,111 -> **36,779** (UX1-7 from 2004-03-26)
  - `vol_indices` 22,968 -> **43,244** (SKEW 1990 … VXEEM/VIX9D 2011)
  - `spx_correlation` 8,605 -> **15,396** (COR1M/3M/6M from 2006)
  - `rates_fx_vol` 8,716 -> **24,842** (MOVE 1988 / JPMVXYG7 1992 / CVIX 2001)

### Phase 3a — per-name deep backfill (begun; newest-first; floor 1994)
- **window 1 (8dc6f59, pushed in the refresh monolith):** vol_iv 2012-07 ->
  2015-01 (+280,302). This is the frozen floor of the refresh-branch monolith
  (vol_iv now 2012-07-02 -> 2026-06-04, 1,668,021 rows, 94.5 MB).
- **windows 2-3 (relocated to the gz buffer branch):** vol_iv 2007-07 -> 2012-06
  (+526,932). **The 2008 GFC IV is captured.** Stored as
  `data/bloomberg/deep/sp500_vol_iv_full__1994_2012.csv.gz` (526,932 rows,
  2007-07-02 -> 2012-06-29, 436 tickers, 8.7 MB gz) on
  `deep-history/bloomberg-raw`. The full uncompressed scratch monolith
  (2007-07 -> 2026-06-04) is also at `C:\Users\mertmert\deep_scratch\` on the box.

## The machinery

`scripts/_bbg_panel.py` (NEW) — shared engine. Fills FORWARD gap [max+1 -> END]
then walks BACKWARD [min-1 -> FLOOR] **newest-first in ~30-month windows**,
rewriting after EVERY window (durable). Per-window: pull members in 30-ticker
`bdh` -> pivot long->wide -> merge+dedupe(date,ticker keep-last) -> sort -> write
-> optional validate() (the OHLCV gate). Pullers are thin configs.

Env knobs: `SWE_PULL_END`, `SWE_PULL_FLOOR`, `SWE_PULL_MODE`
(forward|backfill|both), `SWE_BACKFILL_CHUNK_MONTHS`, `SWE_BACKFILL_MAX_WINDOWS`,
`SWE_OUT_PATH` (write to a scratch path instead of the monolith — used for deep
backfill), `SWE_PULL_LIMIT`, `SWE_PULL_NO_WRITE`.

Cheap-context pullers: `scripts/pull_vix_term_structure.py`,
`scripts/pull_context_index.py <dataset>` (per-ticker pulls so one bad ticker
can't null the batch). Offline tests (`test_pullers_offline.py`, kept off-repo)
prove window tiling + the rotation round-trip/gate. All passed.

## Gotchas

- xbbg 1.2.6 → long narwhals; `.to_native()` + `pivot_table`; `PYTHONUTF8=1`.
- Historical windows use **today's** SPX members (post-IPO names absent in old
  windows; departed names keep old rows). Survivorship is inherent.
- Meter: ~3-6M points this session, **zero throttling** (one 30-month per-name
  window ≈ 1.9M points). Full 3-panel backfill to 1994 ≈ 20-40M → will hit the
  cap; pull-until-error, per-window write keeps what landed, resume.

## RESUME RUNBOOK

Terminal up + logged in; venv active; `cd` repo; `$env:PYTHONUTF8='1'`.

### Continue the deep backfill (vol_iv shown; ohlcv/liquidity analogous)
Grow the off-monolith scratch with `SWE_OUT_PATH`, then re-carve the gz and push
to the buffer branch. The refresh-branch monolith is NEVER advanced.
```
$env:SWE_OUT_PATH='C:\Users\mertmert\deep_scratch\sp500_vol_iv_full.csv'
$env:SWE_PULL_MODE='backfill'; $env:SWE_BACKFILL_MAX_WINDOWS='2'
python scripts/pull_vol_iv.py            # grows scratch 2004..2007, etc.
# re-carve deep slice (date < 2012-07-02) -> data/bloomberg/deep/*.csv.gz, then:
git checkout deep-history/bloomberg-raw
git add -f data/bloomberg/deep/sp500_vol_iv_full__1994_2012.csv.gz
git commit -m "deep: vol_iv backfill chunk" ; git push origin deep-history/bloomberg-raw
git checkout data/bloomberg-refresh-2026-06-02
```
For OHLCV/liquidity: first SEED the scratch from the frozen monolith
(`copy data\bloomberg\sp500_ohlcv.csv <scratch>`), then same loop with
`SWE_OUT_PATH=<scratch>` and a deep filename `sp500_ohlcv__1994_2018.csv.gz` /
`sp500_liquidity__1994_2015.csv.gz`. (OHLCV scratch keeps the rotated map; its
gate still applies.)

### Not done this session (lower urgency / bespoke)
- **Phase 2c snapshots** (`sp500_fundamentals`, `credit_risk`, `institutional`,
  `analyst`, `iv_snapshot_today`): point-in-time `bdp`; re-pull anytime with the
  prior session's verified field lists.
- **sp500_macro:** stale to 2026-03-20; OHLC-per-instrument; refresh + backfill
  to 1990 (+ IG/HY OAS + financial-conditions proxy if entitled). Verify the
  per-instrument bbg map on one instrument first.
- **Phase 4 IV moneyness/skew surface:** `sp500_iv_surface.csv`,
  `{30,60,90}DAY_IMPVOL_{90,95,100,105,110}.0%MNY_DF`, LONG
  `date,ticker,tenor_days,moneyness_pct,iv`. Verify the field floor on one
  ticker; prioritise put side + crisis windows.

## DEFER to laptop/overnight (NOT the metered terminal)
Move the deep gz files from `deep-history/bloomberg-raw` into **Google Drive**
(permanent home); convert to Parquet partitioned by year; add the connector
read-path that assembles recent(git monolith) + deep(Drive); then delete the gz
buffer branch. That connector wiring is the real fix and is out of scope here.
Also deferred (wrong channel): option chains (Theta), dealer GEX (subscription),
`borrow_rate_net` + macro `importance`/deep-history (BQL/Excel), `treasury_yields`
(yfinance).
